generatedSentence: Our soldier, a former journalist with a quiet tendency, felt a nervous jitter as he adjusted the kerchief at his neck. His antique timepiece, a relic from a forgotten institution, ticked rhythmically on the polished tabletop beside a small, potted flower. He was reviewing an old application for a grand renovation of the city atrium, a project that promised inclusive community spaces but brought a strange sense of isolation. He possessed specialized knowledge. His knowledge led him to a jutting fence surrounding the city's old aquarium, now a makeshift stronghold for a barbarian force. A hidden landmine was placed strategically. He watched as a burly barbarian was kicking a metal kettle in frustration near a stall selling pineapple. This brutal feature highlighted their chaotic methods. The soldier knew his supplementary skills as a former sailor would be tested, his preference for stealth clashing with the impending battle. A nearby battery powered a crude alarm system. The battle erupted, a true firestorm of chaos. The enemy jackal leader, clad in thick leather armor, emerged from the stronghold, an iconic figure of terror. He moved with primordial speed, aiming to perforate the soldier's kidney with a gleaming blade. The soldier, with tenacious resolve, parried, the metal texture of his shield scraping against the chieftain's weapon. This was the pinnacle of the fight, a brutal rallye against the eparchy's tyranny. He carried an ancient bible, its pages a subtle feature. The barbarian chieftain was defeated, a victory that would justify the soldier's actions to the legal authorities. The surviving losers from the enemy ranks were rounded up. The once rainy skies cleared, revealing a calm lavender sunset. The city could now indulge in peace, initiating a new era of automation. Though the memory of the battle and its explosion would linger, no more rabies of war would infect the land. The soldier felt a sense of relief, having helped reinforce the fragile peace. He watched an aboriginal child playing badminton, a symbol of renewed hope, knowing the femur of tyranny was broken. The hidden truths were finally exhumed, causing much embarrassment to the old regime.

His antique **timepiece**, a relic from a forgotten **institution**, ticked rhythmically on the polished **tabletop** beside a small, potted **flower**. He was reviewing an old **application** for a grand **renovation** of the city **atrium**, a project that promised **inclusive** community spaces but brought a strange sense of **isolation**. He possessed specialized **knowledge**.

His **knowledge** led him to a **jutting** **fence** surrounding the city's old **aquarium**, now a makeshift stronghold for a **barbarian** force. A hidden **landmine** was placed strategically. The **soldier** knew his **supplementary** **skills** as a former **sailor** would be tested, his **preference** for stealth clashing with the impending **battle**. A nearby **battery** powered a crude alarm system.

The **battle** erupted, a true **firestorm** of chaos. The enemy **jackal** leader, clad in thick **leather** armor, emerged from the stronghold, an **iconic** figure of terror. He moved with **primordial** speed, aiming to **perforate** the **soldier's** **kidney** with a gleaming blade. imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, dynamic superhero action style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A lone soldier, wearing a dark olive tactical suit with subtle lavender piping, a functional kerchief tied around his neck, and a high-tech timepiece on his wrist. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A determined soldier carefully examining an old blueprint on a cluttered tabletop, a single potted flower in the foreground, looking towards a grand atrium window, eye-level shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The same soldier, hidden in shadow, peering through a gap in a jagged fence at a barbarian kicking a metal kettle near a pineapple stand, a visible landmine half-buried, medium shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The soldier in a fierce, dynamic battle with a jackal-headed barbarian chieftain clad in leather armor, swords clashing, an explosion of energy behind them, low-angle action shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The soldier stands on a rooftop overlooking a peaceful city at lavender sunset, watching an aboriginal child joyfully playing badminton in the foreground, wide shot. cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. NO text, NO speech bubbles, NO dialogue.

definitions.definition: A person who loses; one who fails to win or thrive.

definitions.definition: To absolve, and declare to be free of blame or sin.

definitions.definition: A kettle hole, sometimes any pothole.

definitions.definition: To fight or struggle; to enter into a battle with.
definitions.example: She has been battling cancer for years.

definitions.definition: A device used to power electric devices, consisting of a set of electrically connected electrochemical or, archaically, electrostatic cells. A single such cell when used by itself.
The infliction of unlawful physical violence on a person, legally distinguished from assault, which includes the threat of impending violence.
A coordinated group of artillery weapons.
An elevated platform on which cannon could be placed.
An array of similar things.
definitions.example: Schoolchildren take a battery of standard tests to measure their progress.

definitions.definition: A set of small cages where hens are kept for the purpose of farming their eggs.
The catcher and the pitcher together
Two or more major pieces on the same rank, file, or diagonal
A marching percussion ensemble; a drumline.
The state of a firearm when it is possible to be fired.
Apparatus for preparing or serving meals.
An exemplar of the Bible.
A comprehensive manual that describes something. (e.g., handyman’s bible).
(at certain US universities) A compilation of problems and solutions from previous years of a given course, used by some students to cheat on tests or assignments.
Omasum, the third compartment of the stomach of ruminants
A piece of soft sandstone used for scouring the wooden decks of ships, usually with sand and seawater.
A stone with a naturally-formed hole, used by Yorkshiremen for good luck.
Relating to, or having the characteristics of, an icon.
Famously and distinctively representative of its type.
Representing something; symbolic.
definitions.example: an iconic gesture in sign language

definitions.definition: (often followed by "in"): To yield to a temptation or desire.
definitions.example: He looked at the chocolate but didn't indulge.

definitions.definition: To satisfy the wishes or whims of.
definitions.example: Grandma indulges the kids with sweets.

definitions.definition: To give way to (a habit or temptation); not to oppose or restrain.
definitions.example: to indulge sloth, pride, selfishness, or inclinations

definitions.definition: To grant an extension to the deadline of a payment.
To grant as by favour; to bestow in concession, or in compliance with a wish or request.
A custom or practice of a society or community.
An organization similarly long established and respected, particularly one involved with education, public service, or charity work.
The building or buildings which house such an organization.
definitions.example: He's been in an institution since the crash.

definitions.definition: Other places or businesses similarly long established and respected.
definitions.example: Over time, the local pub has become something of an institution.

definitions.definition: A person similarly long established in a place, position, or field.
The act of instituting something.
definitions.example: The institution of higher speed limits was a popular move but increased the severity of crashes.

definitions.definition: The act by which a bishop commits a cure of souls to a priest.
That which institutes or instructs, particularly a textbook or system of elements or rules.
The state of being isolated, detached, or separated.
The state of being away from other people.
The act of isolating.
definitions.example: Social distancing is social isolation, which is a human rights violation on par with torture and other war crimes.

definitions.definition: (of a country) The state of not having diplomatic relations with other countries (either with most or all other countries, or with specified other countries).
The obtaining of an element from one of its compounds, or of a compound from a mixture
The separation of a patient, suffering from a contagious disease, from contact with others (compare: quarantine)
A database property that determines when and how changes made in one transaction are visible to other concurrent transactions.
Including (almost) everything within its scope.
definitions.example: an inclusive list of data formats

definitions.definition: Including the extremes as well as the area between.
definitions.example: numbers 1 to 10 inclusive

definitions.definition: Of, or relating to the first-person plural pronoun when including the person being addressed.
definitions.example: The pronoun in "If you want, we could go back to my place for coffee" is an inclusive "we".
